title: bromus squarrosus subvar. villosus
intro: Bromus squarrosus subvar. villosus, also known as the hairy meadow brome, is a sub-species of the meadow brome plant, a type of grass that belongs to the Poaceae family. This plant is rich in medicinal properties, particularly in its essential oils, which have been traditionally used to treat various health issues. The hairy meadow brome has been found to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, offering several health benefits, including reducing fever and alleviating respiratory problems. The herbal preparations of Bromus squarrosus subvar. villosus include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
